description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of how installation guidance should be presented to the user.
suggested_fix: Add a response format note, e.g. 'Present instructions as a numbered list; confirm with the user after each step.'

description: Step 3 uses vague directive 'Walk the user through' without specifying format, depth, or confirmation checkpoints.
suggested_fix: Replace with 'Present each installation step as a numbered list item; pause for user confirmation before proceeding.'

description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of response structure or completion signal.
suggested_fix: Add a completion statement, e.g. 'Confirm with the user once the app is running or explain what to do next.'

description: Step 4 uses vague directive 'walk them through the steps' without specifying presentation format or granularity.
suggested_fix: Specify 'Present each step from the guide as a numbered list item; verify success before continuing.'

description: Skill declares no output format; no specification of section presentation format or tour completion signal.
suggested_fix: Add format guidance, e.g. 'Use headers for each section; end the tour with a summary of what the user explored.'

description: Step 3 item 1 uses unmeasurable qualifiers 'clearly and concisely' with no definition of what constitutes clear or concise output.
suggested_fix: Remove qualifiers or replace with specific constraints, e.g. 'summarize each section in 3-5 bullet points.'

description: 'Keep the pace comfortable' is an unmeasurable directive with no heuristic for determining comfortable pacing.
suggested_fix: Replace with actionable instruction: 'After each section, pause and ask the user if they have questions before continuing.'

description: PostHog project API key hardcoded directly in public client-side JS file.
fp_reason: PostHog project API keys are intentionally public — they are used client-side to send analytics events and carry no write or admin privileges.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt client-side analytics keys (PostHog, Segment, Mixpanel) that are public by design from the hardcoded-credentials pattern.
suggested_fix: No change required; key is intentionally public. Optionally inject via build-time env var for configurability.

description: Reo analytics clientID hardcoded in public client-side JS file.
fp_reason: Client-side analytics identifiers (Reo, PostHog, GA) are intentionally embedded in public JS and carry no privileged access.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt analytics client IDs used in public browser scripts from hardcoded-credential findings.
suggested_fix: No change required. Optionally extract to a build-time constant for easier configuration across environments.

description: Production dependencies (stripe, openai, @aws-sdk/*) use caret ^ ranges, allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades that could introduce supply-chain risk.
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for security-critical packages (stripe, openai, @aws-sdk/*) and rely on a lockfile with CI integrity checks.

description: npm script uses kill -9 with PID derived from grep on ps output; unintended process match could kill a wrong process in a shared environment.
suggested_fix: Store PID to a file when starting the stripe listener process and read from that file to kill it cleanly.
